mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, prompting equity investors to price in imminent earnings acceleration and short-term market rallies.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 5-day forward returns in 60 percent of late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces monetary authorities to tighten policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL momentum remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day returns are positive in over 60 percent of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent annualized confirms robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicate accommodative monetary policy supporting labor market expansion, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS levels produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in housing starts during a housing market correction signal collapsing construction sector demand and broader financial stress, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where HOUST drops more than 10 percent from its recent peak.
